description: Perform a bootstrap and permutation inference analysis on the 'birthwt' dataset (MASS package) to compare resampling-based inference with classical parametric methods. Implement the analysis in /home/ga/RProjects/bootstrap_analysis.R using the 'boot' package. Save 'bootstrap_ci.csv' to /home/ga/RProjects/output/ containing 95% Normal, Percentile, and BCa confidence intervals (R=2000+) for mean_bwt_smoke, mean_bwt_nosmoke, diff_smoke (non-smoker - smoker), or_smoke, median_bwt, and cor_age_bwt (Columns: statistic, observed, boot_se, ci_normal_lower, ci_normal_upper, ci_percentile_lower, ci_percentile_upper, ci_bca_lower, ci_bca_upper). Save 'permutation_tests.csv' to the same directory with results for smoke_bwt, ht_bwt, ui_bwt, and race_bwt (Columns: test_name, observed_stat, p_value_permutation, p_value_classical, n_permutations, conclusion). Generate a 'parametric_vs_bootstrap.csv' comparison table (Columns: statistic, classical_lower, classical_upper, bca_lower, bca_upper, ci_width_classical, ci_width_bca, relative_width) and a 'bootstrap_figures.png' multi-panel plot visualizing the bootstrap distributions for 'diff_smoke' and 'or_smoke', the permutation null distribution for 'smoke_bwt', and a comparison of Classical vs Bootstrap CIs.
